description:
You are a build-engineer who needs to publish a short, human-readable digest of the
  artifacts generated by the nightly build.

  The directory
    /home/user/builds
  already contains a CSV file named
    artifact_manifest.csv

  • The file has four columns in the following fixed order:
      1. build_id
      2. artifact_name
      3. version
      4. checksum

  • The first row is a header using the exact column names above, separated by commas.
  • All remaining rows are the actual records, also comma-separated.
  • There are no embedded commas or quotes in any field.

  Your task:
  1. Extract only the “artifact_name” and “checksum” columns from
       /home/user/builds/artifact_manifest.csv
     (skip the header row).
  2. Write the result to a new file named
       /home/user/builds/artifact_digests.txt
     using **exactly** the following output format so that automated tests can verify it:
        • Each line contains the artifact_name, then one single space, then the checksum.
        • Preserve the original order in which the records appear in the CSV file.
        • Do not include the header line.
        • Terminate every line (including the last one) with a Unix newline character (“\n”).
  3. Accomplish the whole extraction with one single terminal command (a simple pipeline is fine).
     Do not create any intermediate files.

  When you are finished, the only new file in /home/user/builds should be
    artifact_digests.txt
  with the exact format described above.
truth:
Initial files before the agent starts:

  Path: /home/user/builds/artifact_manifest.csv
  Contents (including the final newline):
    build_id,artifact_name,version,checksum
    101,core-lib,1.0.0,2873acb
    102,ui-assets,1.0.1,9fbc211
    103,service-bin,2.0.0,17ab899

  Files that **must** exist when the task is completed:

  1) Path: /home/user/builds/artifact_digests.txt
     Required contents (including the final newline):
       core-lib 2873acb
       ui-assets 9fbc211
       service-bin 17ab899

  Verification logic:
    • Check that /home/user/builds/artifact_digests.txt exists.
    • Confirm that its SHA256 hash matches the hash of the required contents above.
    • Ensure no other new files were created inside /home/user/builds.

  Notes for the test harness:
    • The agent does not need root privileges.
    • Standard umask will give writable permissions to user; no additional setup required.
